# Solidity Style Guide

> A practical Solidity style guide for writing consistent, safe, and gas-aware smart contracts. Targets Solidity 0.8.34 with coverage of ERC-7201 namespaced storage, transient storage, custom errors in `require`, named imports, NatSpec, and Foundry testing conventions.

The canonical source is `README.md`. This file is a machine-friendly index for LLMs.

## Docs

- [README](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Aboudjem/solidity-style-guide/main/README.md): full guide with ✅ / ❌ Solidity examples for every rule.
- [AGENTS.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Aboudjem/solidity-style-guide/main/AGENTS.md): instructions for coding agents editing or applying the guide.
- [CLAUDE.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Aboudjem/solidity-style-guide/main/CLAUDE.md): Claude Code–specific notes.
- [CONTRIBUTING](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Aboudjem/solidity-style-guide/main/CONTRIBUTING.md): how to propose new rules.
- [SECURITY](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Aboudjem/solidity-style-guide/main/SECURITY.md): disclosure policy.

## Tooling configs (copy-paste ready)

- [.solhint.json](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Aboudjem/solidity-style-guide/main/.solhint.json): Solhint rules matching the guide.
- [.prettierrc](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Aboudjem/solidity-style-guide/main/.prettierrc): prettier-plugin-solidity config (120 cols, 4 spaces, double quotes).
- [.editorconfig](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Aboudjem/solidity-style-guide/main/.editorconfig): editor defaults.

## Skill package

- [skill/SKILL.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Aboudjem/solidity-style-guide/main/skill/SKILL.md): Claude Code skill for applying the guide on any Solidity repo.

## Core rules (one-liners)

- Use the latest stable Solidity (**0.8.34** at time of writing).
- Pin `pragma solidity 0.8.34;` in deployable contracts; use `^0.8.20` in libraries.
- Use **named imports**: `import {X} from "./X.sol";`.
- Use **custom errors**, including inside `require(cond, CustomError())` (≥ 0.8.26).
- **ERC-7201 namespaced storage** for upgradeable contracts.
- Always write explicit types: `uint256`, not `uint`.
- Internal / private identifiers start with `_`.
- Constants `SNAKE_UPPER_CASE`, private constants `_SNAKE_UPPER_CASE`.
- Max line length 120, 4-space indent, LF, UTF-8.
- One contract / interface / library per file, name matching filename.
- Foundry test naming: `test_Description`, `testFuzz_Description`, `test_RevertWhen_Description`, `invariant_Property`.
